Key Takeaways:
• Other types of Prelaunch content.
o Audio - generally not recommended, but can be done
▪ You can turn it into screen capture and it will raise perceived value
dramatically
o PDF reports
▪ Effective especially when combined with video
▪ Can be a written report, process map, mind map, flowchart,
infographic
▪ High perceived value, can be great for getting opt ins
o Webinar or teleseminar
▪ Can use this during your prelaunch or after you open cart (I like to
use it after open cart)
• Freeconferencecalling.com
• InstantTeleseminar.com
• GoToWebinar.com
o Software
▪ You can give away software as a piece of prelaunch content
▪ Can be very effective - but not something I’ve personally done
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Summary & Things to Remember:
• “Horses for courses” - what you use depends on your situation and what your
personal assets are
• Video is really great for lot of situations - it's good for you to get comfortable with
video
• If you use video show your personality, be natural, be human
• With video it's a lot easier to appear human and be approachable/ personable than
it is for most people to do in writing
• The camera is not important
• Audio quality is important
• Use the minimum tools necessary to get the job done
• It is about progress, it's not about perfection
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 6 --

4
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Now we are talking about creating other types of Prelaunch Content pretty
quickly here.
There are lots of other types of Prelaunch Content in addition to video. One of
them is audio, but this is generally not recommended.
Back in the old days we used audio a lot. If you have a great audio and maybe you
did an audio interview with someone that would be great or you got a few different audio
interviews and you're going to put them together into one audio.
You can use that, but you want to turn it into screen capture video. Take that
audio and build in some slides for it, build a mind map for it, get some photos or
pictures, and marry up that audio to the screen capture and it will raise the perceived
value in a huge way.
Generally, I'm not a big fan of audio these days, it's very rare for people to come
to a website, sit there and click a play button and listen to an audio. Turn it into a video
and it will really be a lot more impactful.
You can do a pdf reports. You create a document, turn it into a pdf, which you
can do with pretty much all kinds of software these days. That feature is built right into
most word processing software pages for the Mac or Microsoft Word, very easy to create
pdf.
You can do it as a written report where you write a short white paper type of
document.
You can create a process map, a mind map, a flow chart, an info
graphic. The great thing about pdfs is they have really high perceived value and people
will opt in if you tell them they can download a pdf, they will opt in for it. Really works
very, very well.
Another thing that works great is mixing a pdf with a video. And I do that
with my Product Launch Blueprint. I'll have a video with me going through the actual
pdf. The other thing is, is it doesn't have to be super long. I've knocked out a great pdf
report in a matter of few hours. If you can do a pdf that's six to eight pages and then you
put in some graphics and a title page and a conclusion you can end up with a twelve,
fourteen, sixteen-page report pretty darn quickly. Pdfs; are a very high perceived value.
It could be very, very effective especially when you match them with videos like I
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 6 --

5
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
do. If you put those two together, in terms of getting people to opt in to your Prelaunch
Content, that is a winning strategy.
Now there are even more kinds of Prelaunch Content, such as webinars or
teleseminars.
They can be used during your Prelaunch or they could be used after you open
your cart. I tend to use them more after I open my cart or right at the end of my
Prelaunch, right before we are about to open a because a webinar means that they can
see your screen and you end up with a presentation that's not unlike a screen capture.
The difference with a webinar is you're delivering that live and people are all dialed in at
that one time for the webinar. Teleseminar is purely via the telephone; they are only
hearing it via audio.
A lot easier to create a teleseminar than a webinar because you don't have to
bother creating slides or having to worry about what it looks like, so teleseminars can be
very easy.
Freeconferencecalling.com; it's a place you can get what we call a bridge line.
That's how you actually do your teleseminar. You will dial into the service from your
phone and you've got a pin code that you type in and that makes you the moderator so
you can speak then other people can dial in. They enter a pin code as an attendee so they
can listen. Very, very simple to use; very easy to use. Freeconferencecalling.com you can
actually get a free, what we call bridge line that allows you to do that.
If you're going to do a webinar, you can use Instant Teleseminar or you can use
GoToWebinar, those are two services. GoToWebinar is generally more flexible, more
powerful and it costs a whole lot more money than Instant Teleseminar. I've used both of
them. They're both very nice services. And then you can also, as Prelaunch, you can give
away software.
This is not something that I have done, but you can actually create
software, probably not the most super-expensive crazy software and you probably just
want to use the software as one piece of Prelaunch Content either for your first piece of
Prelaunch Content or your last piece of Prelaunch Content.
It is really high perceived value. It can be very, very effective. This is not
something I've done. I've helped out on a couple launches where they did it, but I haven't
done this. I'm not much of a software builder at this point in my life so I can't tell you a
lot about this from experience so you're really on your own with this one.
Like I said, I have seen this be very, very effective so if it ties in well with your
launch, just create some piece of software if you have experience or comfort doing that
then it's something to consider.

6
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Circling back, remember in terms of creative Prelaunch Content. It is all about
horses for courses.
What you use depends on your situation and what you can create and what your
assets are. Maybe you just did this amazing interview with some world class expert in
your market that just brings huge authority and social proof to your launch. Now you’ve
got this great audio you turn that into a screen capture video. There you go; boom you've
got it.
On the other hand, maybe you're just really great on camera or you're really well-
spoken on camera, you've got someone that can shoot video for you. Then use a video.
Maybe you're just an incredible writer. You can create incredible reports, use a pdf. What
you use should be based on your situation and what your assets are and what you're
comfortable with.
Video is really great for lots of situations, and it's good for you to get comfortable
with video. Even if you're not comfortable right now, it's a great thing to start working on
because, like I said earlier in the first video about creating Prelaunch Content series,
people can connect with you. It's a lot easier to appear human and appear approachable
and personable and you've got a personality than it is for most people to do in writing.
It's usually easier to do that on video. People can see you. They can sense your
passion, your sincerity so it's great for lots of situations. Even if you're not comfortable
on video now, if you just start shooting now, you'll be comfortable sooner rather than
later. It doesn't that long to get comfortable in front of the camera.
Remember, finally, it is about progress. It's not about perfection.
You will get better at this. Just go ahead and knock it out. Get some Prelaunch
Content done. If it's not perfect then you can always do it over again, but it's really about
progress and getting better over time. I've improved, I've seen so many of my PLF
owners improve over time. It's amazing how you just get better and better by taking
those baby steps and moving forward and increasing skills a little bit at a time.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
